title: Rootless containers with systemd do not start after reboot
body:
### Issue Description

I have a number of containers running as rootless for 'ec2-user' and managed by systemd which are running fine, I can stop, start without issues.
However, a number of them, they will not come up after a reboot.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. podman ps -a

```
[ec2-user@ip-172-31-17-143 ~]$ podman ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE                                                                        COMMAND               CREATED         STATUS             PORTS       NAMES
01c8cb387a47  registry.redhat.io/rhel8/postgresql-13:latest                                run-postgresql        53 minutes ago  Up 7 minutes ago               postgresql
29b70b733f91  registry.redhat.io/rhel8/redis-6:latest                                      run-redis             53 minutes ago  Up 7 minutes ago               redis
3cd1076d90d8  registry.redhat.io/ansible-automation-platform-23/ee-supported-rhel8:latest  /usr/bin/receptor...  52 minutes ago  Up 8 minutes ago               receptor
16e7b8b1df3f  registry.redhat.io/ansible-automation-platform-23/controller-rhel8:latest    /usr/bin/launch_a...  52 minutes ago  Up 7 minutes ago               automation-controller-task
5bbbdd84c837  registry.redhat.io/ansible-automation-platform-23/controller-rhel8:latest    /usr/bin/launch_a...  52 minutes ago  Up 29 seconds ago              automation-controller-web
5082bf4bae64  registry.redhat.io/ansible-automation-platform-23/hub-rhel8:latest           pulp-api              51 minutes ago  Up 20 seconds ago              automation-hub-api
8b28a2a89991  registry.redhat.io/ansible-automation-platform-23/hub-rhel8:latest           pulp-content          51 minutes ago  Up 16 seconds ago              automation-hub-content
3096e7226c2a  registry.redhat.io/ansible-automation-platform-23/hub-web-rhel8:latest       nginx -g daemon o...  51 minutes ago  Up 8 minutes ago               automation-hub-web
38aa251ff2ea  registry.redhat.io/ansible-automation-platform-23/hub-rhel8:latest           pulp-worker           51 minutes ago  Up 7 minutes ago               automation-hub-worker

```
2. systemctl --user status


Some containers that do not start after reboot exhibit this in journalctl:

```
[ec2-user@ip-172-31-17-143 ~]$ sudo journalctl -u automation-controller-web
-- Logs begin at Wed 2023-03-15 10:41:08 UTC, end at Wed 2023-03-15 10:44:21 UTC. --
Mar 15 10:41:14 ip-172-31-17-143.ec2.internal systemd[1]: Starting Podman automation-controller-web.service...
Mar 15 10:41:15 ip-172-31-17-143.ec2.internal podman[1056]: Error: no container with name or ID "automation-controller-web" found: no such container
Mar 15 10:41:15 ip-172-31-17-143.ec2.internal systemd[1]: automation-controller-web.service: Control process exited, code=exited status=125
Mar 15 10:41:16 ip-172-31-17-143.ec2.internal podman[1439]: Error: no container with name or ID "automation-controller-web" found: no such container
Mar 15 10:41:16 ip-172-31-17-143.ec2.internal systemd[1]: automation-controller-web.service: Control process exited, code=exited status=125
Mar 15 10:41:16 ip-172-31-17-143.ec2.internal systemd[1]: automation-controller-web.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.
```

But clearly they do exist:

```
[ec2-user@ip-172-31-17-143 ~]$ podman ps -a |grep automation-controller-web
5bbbdd84c837  registry.redhat.io/ansible-automation-platform-23/controller-rhel8:latest    /usr/bin/launch_a...  57 minutes ago  Exited (137) 3 minutes ago              automation-controller-web
```

If I start manually, they work just fine:

```
[ec2-user@ip-172-31-17-143 ~]$ systemctl --user start automation-controller-web
[ec2-user@ip-172-31-17-143 ~]$ podman ps -a |grep automation-controller-web
5bbbdd84c837  registry.redhat.io/ansible-automation-platform-23/controller-rhel8:latest    /usr/bin/launch_a...  58 minutes ago  Up 2 seconds ago                        automation-controller-web
```

### Describe the results you expected

All containers would be up and running after a reboot and systemctl --user status would not show degraded.

comments.body:
How do your unit files look like? Any chance you can test with the latest podman release?

From podman ps it looks like the containers were actually started:
`Exited (137) About a minute ago` so this sounds like they got killed by something, possibly systemd.

```
[ec2-user@ip-172-31-17-143 ~]$ cat .config/systemd/user/automation-controller-web.service 
# automation-controller-web.service
# autogenerated by Podman 4.2.0
# Wed Mar 15 13:20:48 UTC 2023

[Unit]
Description=Podman automation-controller-web.service
Documentation=man:podman-generate-systemd(1)
Wants=network-online.target
After=network-online.target
RequiresMountsFor=/run/user/1000/containers

[Service]
Environment=PODMAN_SYSTEMD_UNIT=%n
Restart=on-failure
TimeoutStopSec=70
ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman start automation-controller-web
ExecStop=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 automation-controller-web
ExecStopPost=/usr/bin/podman stop -t 10 automation-controller-web
PIDFile=/run/user/1000/containers/overlay-containers/47e06d827c69aa7eaed71a9f77fa8f9e759fa8904e9b917a4c2ae8bb9000ac79/userdata/conmon.pid
Type=forking

[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

Since the log file is complaining about containers not existing, and yet when you login, the containers exist, I would think you are looking at two different databases.  Perhaps when booting it is looking at rootful when  you think it is looking at rootless?  Something in storage.conf is different?

No, I don't have a custom storage.conf for this rootless user, so I assume it just uses system-wide /etc/containers/storage.conf.
